CREATE TABLE inet_example (address inet NOT NULL);
SELECT address FROM inet_example WHERE address = inet '192.168.1.5/24';
CREATE TABLE subnet_example (subnet inet NOT NULL);
SELECT a.address FROM inet_example AS a JOIN subnet_example AS b ON a.address << b.subnet;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM inet_example WHERE address >>= inet '192.168.1.0/24';
SELECT address FROM inet_example WHERE address IN (SELECT subnet FROM subnet_example);
SELECT CAST('10.1.1.15/16' AS inet) AS valid_inet_address;
CREATE TABLE multi_table_example (id INT, address inet NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO multi_table_example VALUES (1, inet '192.168.2.1/24'), (2, inet '10.10.20.1/16');
SELECT m.id, m.address FROM multi_table_example AS m JOIN inet_example AS e ON m.address << e.address WHERE e.address = inet '192.168.0.0/24';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM inet_example WHERE inet '192.168.1.5' << address;
